trajectory.description: Observation: Current best balanced accuracy is 0.8036 (many=0.8035, medium=0.8571, few=0.7432). Few-shot accuracy is the weakest link. Model peaked at epoch 12 then overfit. Analysis: Two key improvements: (1) Balanced Softmax Loss during training adjusts for class prior - should improve few-shot accuracy significantly. (2) Two-stage training (cRT) - representation learning with instance sampling learns better features, then classifier re-training with balanced sampling fixes the head. Instance-balanced sampling is known to learn better features than weighted sampling. Decision: Implement two-stage training: Stage 1 (40 epochs) with balanced softmax + instance sampling + mixup, Stage 2 (15 epochs) with frozen backbone + re-initialized classifier + balanced sampling. Training should take ~22min, well within budget.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best balanced accuracy improved from 0.8036 to 0.8346 with balanced softmax + EMA. Few-shot accuracy (0.7639) is the weakest link. Model peaked at epoch 13 suggesting overfitting. Analysis: Key improvements needed: (1) better regularization (drop path), (2) better LR schedule (cosine with warmup), (3) larger batch size for more stable gradients, (4) label smoothing. The cRT stage 2 hurt performance so I'll skip it and focus on better stage 1 training. Decision: Train ConvNeXt with drop_path=0.1, cosine schedule with warmup, batch_size=128, EMA decay=0.9995, label_smoothing=0.1 for 50 epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best test balanced accuracy is 0.8346 with balanced softmax + EMA. Current training with more regularization (drop_path, label_smoothing, mixup) got only 0.8330 on val - worse. Analysis: Additional regularization may conflict with balanced softmax. Instead of retraining, I can try post-hoc logit adjustment at inference (sweep α in logits + α*log_freq) and τ-normalization of classifier weights. These are free improvements that don't require retraining. Decision: Write and run a sweep script to find optimal α and τ on val set, then update predict.py and submit eval.
